body: CLAIM: There exists a meta-symmetry principle M, not reducible to Born reciprocity or existing phase-space QM formulations, such that imposing M on the quantum-mechanical formalism uniquely determines an operator 𝒯̂, and a Hawking-type exponential map applied to 𝒯̂ yields the functional form of the cosmological dark-energy density ρ_Λ (with its magnitude fixed empirically rather than derived). | VARIABLES: M ranges over candidate symmetry constraints on the QM operator algebra; 𝒯̂ ranges over operators on the system's Hilbert space / observable algebra; exp_H(·) ranges over exponential maps from operators to cosmological density functionals; ρ_Λ ranges over functional forms of the dark-energy density in terms of cosmological parameters; C = {Born reciprocity, existing phase-space QM formulations} is the comparison class for non-equivalence. | REGIME: Standard quantum-mechanical formalism (Hilbert space, operator algebra, Born rule) extended by a putative meta-symmetry; cosmological dark-energy sector where ρ_Λ is an observable density; assumes a well-defined exponential map bridging operator algebra to cosmological density; boundary condition: the magnitude of ρ_Λ is set by empirical observation, only its functional form is derived; scale: from quantum operator level to cosmological density level. | FALSIFIABLE: no
